name: Confluence
description: Search and read Confluence pages and spaces, create and update content, and query your knowledge base using CQL through AI chat.
setup_notes:
Requires an Atlassian Cloud site name, user email, and API token.

Admin setup:
1. Go to https://id.atlassian.com/manage-profile/security/api-tokens
2. Click 'Create API token' and give it a descriptive label (e.g., 'MCP Server - Confluence')
3. Copy the generated token immediately (it won't be shown again)
4. Determine your site name: it's the subdomain of your Confluence URL (e.g., for https://mycompany.atlassian.net/wiki, the site name is 'mycompany')
5. Enter the site name, the email address associated with the Atlassian account, and the API token in the server configuration

Note: This server only supports Confluence Cloud (not Confluence Server/Data Center). The same API token can be used for both Jira and Confluence if they share the same Atlassian Cloud site. Atlassian also offers an official remote MCP server at https://mcp.atlassian.com with OAuth 2.1, but this template uses the community @aashari/mcp-server-atlassian-confluence package for simpler API token-based setup.
